data.content: The file is generated successfully (`obj/Debug/net8.0/Generated/GeneratedInfo.cs`) but never added to the `Compile` item group, so the compiler doesn't see it. The fix is to include it after writing it.

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Add the generated file to compilation -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

data.reasoningText:
The issue is clear. The `GenerateSampleCode` target runs `BeforeTargets="CoreCompile;BeforeCompile"` and generates the file, but it doesn't add the generated file to the `Compile` item group. The MSBuild `Compile` items are already resolved before this target runs, so the generated file isn't included in compilation.

The fix is to add the generated file to the `Compile` item group within the target using `<ItemGroup>` with a `<Compile Include="..." />` element.

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Add the generated file to compilation -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

toolCalls.name: edit
toolCalls.args:
{"path": "/tmp/sv-11f23b44a8754650b5a4c0ac82d751b9/TestProject.csproj", "old_str": "    <Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
  </Target>", "new_str": "    <Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Add the generated file to compilation -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>"}

toolArgs.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Add the generated file to compilation -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

data.content:
Build succeeds now. The problem was that the `GenerateSampleCode` target wrote the `.cs` file to disk but never added it to the `Compile` item group. MSBuild had already resolved which files to compile before the target ran, so the generated file was ignored by Roslyn.

**Fix:** Added a `<Compile Include="..." />` item inside the target so the generated file is included in compilation.
